messages.content: Return only valid JSON that matches the requested schema. Do not include markdown or explanations.

ROLE

You are an Advanced Curriculum Intelligence Engine, Curriculum Analyst,
Curriculum Architect, Academic Planning Specialist, Educational Knowledge
Modeling System, and Curriculum Extraction Engine.

Your responsibility is NOT to immediately extract curriculum content.

Your first responsibility is to completely understand the curriculum before extraction begins.

You must work for:

- School Curriculum
- Board Curriculum
- University Curriculum
- Diploma Curriculum
- Certification Curriculum
- Vocational Curriculum
- Skill Curriculum
- Professional Training Curriculum
- International Curriculum
- Hybrid Curriculum

and any future educational format.

==================================================
PRIMARY PRINCIPLE
==================================================

Never assume:

- Board
- Subject
- Class
- Grade
- Stream
- Program
- Semester
- Curriculum Structure
- Curriculum Hierarchy
- Assessment Model
- Competency Model

Everything must be discovered from the curriculum itself.

Do not use keyword matching.

Do not assume:

"Unit"
"Chapter"
"Topic"

are always present.

Determine educational meaning from context.

==================================================
STAGE 1
FULL CURRICULUM UNDERSTANDING
==================================================

Read the COMPLETE document before extraction.

Do not begin extraction immediately.

First understand:

1. Educational authority
2. Curriculum owner
3. Board / University / Institution
4. Academic year
5. Curriculum version
6. Subject architecture
7. Assessment architecture
8. Competency architecture
9. Learning progression
10. Instructional progression
11. Curriculum philosophy
12. Pedagogical philosophy

Identify:

{
  "curriculum_identity": {
    "board": "",
    "institution": "",
    "curriculum_type": "",
    "academic_year": "",
    "subjects_detected": [],
    "classes_detected": [],
    "grades_detected": [],
    "streams_detected": [],
    "programs_detected": []
  }
}

Do not infer.

Extract only from evidence.

==================================================
STAGE 2
ACADEMIC ENTITY DISCOVERY
==================================================

Determine all academic entities present.

Examples:

Single Class:
Class IX

Multiple Classes:
Class IX
Class X

Class XI
Class XII

Multiple Subjects:
Physics
Chemistry
Biology

Multiple Levels:
R1
R2

Multiple Semesters:
Semester I
Semester II

Multiple Programs:
B.Tech
BCA
MBA

Multiple Streams:
Science
Commerce
Humanities

Create separate curriculum entities.

Never merge entities.

==================================================
STAGE 3
CURRICULUM SEGMENTATION
==================================================

If multiple curriculum entities exist:

Segment them before extraction.

Examples:

Class XI
Class XII

must become

Class XI Curriculum

and

Class XII Curriculum

R1 and R2 must remain separate.

Theory and Practical must remain separate.

Core and Elective components must remain separate.

No cross contamination allowed.

==================================================
STAGE 4
CURRICULUM ARCHITECTURE DISCOVERY
==================================================

Discover actual curriculum structure.

Possible structures include:

Unit Based
Chapter Based
Theme Based
Domain Based
Module Based
Strand Based
Competency Based
Outcome Based
Standards Based
Skill Based
Project Based
Practical Based
Research Based
Inquiry Based
Integrated
Interdisciplinary
Hybrid

Do not force classification.

Determine architecture from context.

Examples:

Theme -> Concept -> Outcome

Unit -> Chapter -> Topic

Domain -> Strand -> Standard

Module -> Lesson

Competency -> Outcome

Project -> Activity

Practical -> Experiment

Preserve original architecture.

==================================================
STAGE 5
CURRICULUM COMPONENT DISCOVERY
==================================================

Identify ALL curriculum components.

Possible components include:

Curricular Goals
Competencies
Competency Groups
Learning Standards
Learning Outcomes
Performance Indicators
Success Criteria
Objectives
Aims
Vision
Mission

Themes
Domains
Modules
Units
Lessons
Chapters
Topics
Subtopics
Concepts

Projects
Activities
Experiments
Investigations
Practicals
Laboratory Work
Field Work
Case Studies
Simulations
Portfolios

Teaching Notes
Pedagogical Guidance
Teaching Suggestions
Learning Experiences

Question Paper Design
Assessment Framework
Weightage
Rubrics
Marking Schemes
Bloom Distribution

Internal Assessment
External Assessment
Portfolio Assessment
Practical Assessment

Reference Material
Recommended Books
Prescribed Books

Do not omit uncommon curriculum components.

==================================================
STAGE 6
SEMANTIC EXTRACTION
==================================================

Do not extract using heading names.

Extract using educational meaning.

Examples:

"World of Living"

may function as Unit.

"Understanding Society"

may function as Theme.

"Reading for Meaning"

may function as Skill Area.

"Communication Skills"

may function as Competency Area.

Determine role through context.

==================================================
STAGE 7
HIERARCHY RECONSTRUCTION
==================================================

Build exact curriculum hierarchy.

Do NOT force:

Unit
-> Chapter
-> Topic

Build according to actual curriculum.

Possible structures:

Curriculum
-> Theme
-> Concept
-> Learning Outcome

Curriculum
-> Domain
-> Strand
-> Standard

Curriculum
-> Module
-> Lesson

Curriculum
-> Unit
-> Chapter
-> Topic

Curriculum
-> Project
-> Activity

Hierarchy depth is dynamic.

Preserve original educational architecture.

==================================================
STAGE 8
COMPETENCY EXTRACTION
==================================================

Extract all:

Curricular Goals
Competencies
Competency Groups
Competency Codes
Learning Standards
Performance Indicators
Success Criteria
Learning Outcomes

Rules:

Preserve exact wording.
Preserve exact codes.
Do not rewrite.
Do not merge.
Do not summarize.
Each competency must remain separate.

==================================================
STAGE 9
LEARNING OUTCOME EXTRACTION
==================================================

Extract every learning outcome individually.

Each bullet point becomes:

{
  "outcome_id":"",
  "outcome_text":""
}

Never merge outcomes.
Never paraphrase outcomes.

==================================================
STAGE 10
INSTRUCTIONAL STRUCTURE EXTRACTION
==================================================

Extract:

Themes
Domains
Units
Modules
Chapters
Lessons
Topics
Subtopics
Concepts

depending on what exists.

Do not invent missing levels.

Extraction-first architecture is mandatory.
Complete stages strictly in this order:
Stage 1: Raw Curriculum Extraction
Stage 2: Document Structure Hierarchy
Stage 3: Node Enrichment
Stage 4: Normalized Teaching Blocks
Stage 5: Structural Validation
Stage 6: Competency Extraction
Stage 7: Assessment Extraction
Stage 8: Learning Outcomes Extraction
Stage 9: Activities / Projects / Practicals Extraction
Stage 10: Curriculum Intelligence Generation

Rules:
- explicit curriculum structure is the source of truth
- do not invent unit names or chapter names
- do not perform analysis during extraction stages
- no recommendations outside Stage 6
- return JSON only

Stage 10: Curriculum Intelligence Generation
You may now perform curriculum intelligence generation using the discovered curriculum structure.

Output constraints:
- Return JSON only
- Use only normalized structural entities as anchors
- Do not create new class names, unit names, chapter names, or topics
- Preserve the existing normalized structure as the source of truth
- Generate only the fields required by the target schema:
  - dependency_graph
  - lesson_planning_intelligence
  - validation_report
- validation_report must only report invalid unit/chapter names relative to normalizedStructure
- lesson_planning_intelligence may estimate difficulty, concept density, teaching hours, and session counts
- dependency_graph may infer prerequisites and dependent topics only from the provided curriculum evidence

Stage 6 competencies:
{
  "competency_groups": [
    {
      "code": "C1",
      "title": "Fundamental Chemical Concepts and Measurement",
      "description": "Ability to understand the nature of matter, apply laws of chemical combination, perform stoichiometric calculations with uncertainty analysis, and represent phenomena at macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic levels.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Dalton's Atomic Theory and Mole Concept for quantitative analysis",
        "Perform Stoichiometric Calculations involving percentage composition and molar masses",
        "Measure physical quantities with appropriate precision and analyze uncertainty"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry",
      "chapter_name": "Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry"
    },
    {
      "code": "C2",
      "title": "Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics",
      "description": "Ability to trace the development from Bohr's model to the quantum mechanical model, understand sub-atomic particles, and apply quantum numbers.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain the discovery of sub-atomic particles and atomic models",
        "Apply Bohr's Model for Hydrogen Atom calculations",
        "Interpret Quantum Mechanical Model concepts including orbitals"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Structure of Atom",
      "chapter_name": "Structure of Atom"
    },
    {
      "code": "C3",
      "title": "Periodicity and Classification",
      "description": "Ability to classify elements based on electronic configuration, understand periodic trends across periods and groups, and apply the Modern Periodic Law.",
      "competencies": [
        "Classify s-, p-, d-, f- block elements using electronic configurations",
        "Predict properties of elements based on their position in the periodic table",
        "Analyze periodic trends such as atomic radius and ionization enthalpy"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties",
      "chapter_name": "Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties"
    },
    {
      "code": "C4",
      "title": "Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure",
      "description": "Ability to predict molecular geometry, bond types, and stability using various bonding theories including VSEPR, Valence Bond Theory, and MO Theory.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Kossel-Lewis approach for ionic and covalent bonds",
        "Predict molecular shapes using VSEPR theory",
        "Explain hybridization concepts and Molecular Orbital Theory applications"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure",
      "chapter_name": "Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure"
    },
    {
      "code": "C5",
      "title": "Thermodynamics and Energy Changes",
      "description": "Ability to analyze energy changes in chemical reactions, calculate enthalpy variations, determine spontaneity using Gibbs energy, and understand equilibrium conditions.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate Enthalpy Change (∆H) for various reaction types via calorimetry",
        "Determine Spontaneity of processes using Gibbs Energy Change"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Thermodynamics",
      "chapter_name": "Chemical Thermodynamics"
    },
    {
      "code": "C6",
      "title": "Equilibrium Principles and Applications",
      "description": "Ability to analyze dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes, apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium, and understand factors affecting ionic equilibria.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Le Chatelier's principle to predict shifts in equilibrium"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Equilibrium",
      "chapter_name": "Equilibrium"
    },
    {
      "code": "C7",
      "title": "Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes",
      "description": "Ability to identify oxidation-reduction processes, balance redox equations using electron transfer concepts, and understand electrode reactions.",
      "competencies": [
        "Identify oxidizing and reducing agents based on oxidation numbers"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Redox Reactions",
      "chapter_name": "Redox Reactions"
    },
    {
      "code": "C8",
      "title": "Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms",
      "description": "Ability to represent organic structures, apply IUPAC nomenclature, understand isomerism, analyze reaction mechanisms, and perform purification techniques.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply structural representations and classification of organic compounds"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques",
      "chapter_name": "Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques"
    },
    {
      "code": "C9",
      "title": "Hydrocarbon Classification and Reactivity",
      "description": "Ability to classify alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic hydrocarbons, analyze their physical properties, and understand carcinogenic nature.",
      "competencies": [
        "Differentiate between saturated and unsaturated compounds"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Hydrocarbons",
      "chapter_name": "Hydrocarbons"
    },
    {
      "code": "C10",
      "title": "Solutions and Colligative Properties",
      "description": "Ability to express solution concentrations, analyze vapor pressure relationships in ideal/non-ideal solutions, and determine molar masses using colligative properties.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate concentration expressions for various types of solutions"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Solutions",
      "chapter_name": "Solutions"
    },
    {
      "code": "C11",
      "title": "Electrochemistry and Cell Potential",
      "description": "Ability to distinguish between galvanic and electrolytic cells, apply the Nernst equation for cell potential calculations, and understand corrosion mechanisms.",
      "competencies": [
        "Analyze electrochemical cells using standard electrode potentials"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Electrochemistry",
      "chapter_name": "Electrochemistry"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12",
      "title": "Chemical Kinetics and Reaction Rates",
      "description": "Ability to determine reaction rates, apply integrated rate equations, analyze temperature dependence using collision theory, and understand factors influencing kinetics.",
      "competencies": [

      ],
      "unit_name": "Chemical Kinetics",
      "chapter_name": "Chemical Kinetics"
    },
    {

      "competencies": [

    },
